transcript.clean_text: two mountain ranges down two still to go but a day of transition for the peloton of the tour de france after the abdues stage that saw thomas pidcock take his first big win on the road a tour de france debut and for the mountain bike olympic champion world champion on the crossfield a tour de france stage win at a classic mountain top finish and the gc unchanged amongst the top three with only human balde losing places he dropped from second to fourth bulgarcha back where he belonged garrett thomas the formal winner now in third sun rose on another day and a day away from the alps heading towards sante tien the 27th time that the tour de france would stop over in the big southeast city 192.6 kilometers just over 2000 meters of climbing a funny profile one lending itself to either the sprinters or the breakaway riders as is habitual in the tour in the last few days it began with a really fast start it took until the first classified climb 30 kilometers in for any sort of break to go philippe organa went away with jorgensen king was also there and then there was a mighty group chasing behind splits as early as that first climb segan jakobson and a few others out the back and alpacinda kerning desperate to bring it back because jasper phillips hadn't missed the move a few did get brought back but it wasn't the end of hostilities fred wright with hugo mas peterson quinn simmons making their way to the front seven up the road and then the wind started to blow the gap as low as one minute 26 at times certain riders being called out including watfornat whilst he was taking a feedback lotto and alpacine were chasing to try and set up a sprint caleb wanting to be there but only finding himself on the ground a key moment in the race with 71 kilometers to go ewen back on his bike but he would not see the main peloton again that would take lotto sodal out of the chase and the chase was losing impetus the final classified climb gave the breakaway another minute three and a half there gap with 44 k's to go it looked as though we were set for the break to go jakobson and other sprinters dropped but bike exchange began to go to work they furiously knocked off a minute off in a space of just a few kilometers but the gap got to two and a half minutes and then wasn't really budging inside 15 kilometers to go behind they sat up matthews maze gets the last riders having a go the search called off the games beginning in the front 12 and a half kilometers remain on the final unclassified rise mass pitherson strong all day managed to make the difference simmons his teammate was already gone by this point he split the group in half gandna surprised whilst he was taking a feed peterson taking right and all with him the three chasers would not see the front again so a trio to take it into the final few hundred meters another fast day on the tour a transition stage they said a transition from a man who had many titles but one missing a grand tour stage winner mars pitterson went after it from the very first pedal strokes and the former world champion now became a tour de france stage winner right in second all in third 45.7 kilometers per hour all the way to santetian amass peterson the winner of a tour of heart stage the strongest all day long the quickest in the sprint what a tour de france for denmark they still have the yellow jersey they have yet another stage win the third in this race so far almost six minutes behind the peloton came home led home by watfornat mopping up more green jersey points peterson winning the stage ahead of right king jorgensen and ghana bernard then in seventh place with cinechell monsanto and pasqualon all on the day's top ten a green jersey again for fanart another day in yellow ticked off for jonas vinegar who retains his two minutes 22 advantage over today pogacha with garrett thomas third balder and yates making up the top five a possible gc day again into monde the massive central our third mountain range a difficult day bringing back memories for omar freilett and steve cummings another good day maybe for the breakaway but at this tour de france you just never know 192 192.5 kilometers you'll better watch all the action as usual live or on demand on gcn plus don't miss it you
